scan_method: PowerShell Get-ChildItem + Get-FileHash for files; inline Python hashlib + os.walk for paired-directory recursive SHA256 comparison (PowerShell Get-FileHash failed on reserved filename 'nul' inside E:\GT-KB\admin\provider\, requiring Python fallback). The Python comparison was executed inline (not as a persisted helper script) per Codex F1 in -004 NO-GO.
deletion_authority_note: STALE-DUPLICATE may be used only when in-root content is proven equal-or-superset by path-relative evidence. Where outside-only paths exist (even when content is mostly nested build residue), classification is DIVERGED. Owner authorizes deletion separately; this manifest records candidates, not authorizations.

candidates.name: admin
candidates.recommended_action: owner inspects diverged + outside-only paths before deletion. Pattern observation: outside-only files are predominantly nested-dist build artifacts (paths like 'shopify/dist/dist/' and 'standalone/dist/dist/' suggest a build was once run inside an existing build directory). The 29 outside-only paths are dated 2026-02-06 or earlier (pre-migration era). In-root admin/ has 44873 additional files spanning the full codebase. Outside content appears redundant but per Codex GO condition 7 cannot be classified STALE-DUPLICATE without strict path-relative subset evidence.

candidates.name: requirements.txt

candidates.name: src
candidates.recommended_action: owner inspects diverged paths and outside-only paths carefully. Pattern observation: E:\src\ contains both top-level chat/, integrations/ AND a NESTED src/chat/, src/integrations/ subtree, suggesting an old corrupted checkout where someone copied the project into its own src/ directory. The outside-only paths include `chat/pipeline.py` (a single non-nested file) which may or may not be unique. Of the 298 outside files: 5 match in-root exactly, 142 share names with in-root but content differs, 151 paths exist only outside. Outside last-modified 2026-02-15 (older); in-root has 587 files (about 2x more). High likelihood the outside copy is fully superseded by in-root development since 2026-02-15, but per Codex GO condition 7 cannot be classified STALE-DUPLICATE without strict path-relative subset proof.

candidates.name: tmp
candidates.recommended_action: owner may authorize deletion after spot-check (recently modified 2026-04-24; 353 files / 6.9 MB; generic temp directory; no in-root counterpart). Recent modification means owner inspection is recommended before deletion is authorized.

candidates.name: tmp-ps

candidates.name: widget
candidates.recommended_action: owner may authorize deletion after spot-checking 1 diverged + 1 outside-only path. Both are widget build artifacts (.iife.js). The outside-only path is `dist/dist/...` (nested-dist pattern matching admin/ and src/). Outside is dated 2026-02-13; in-root widget/ has 15270 files (full source + build pipeline). Content pattern strongly indicates outside is an old standalone build artifact whose role has been taken over by in-root; owner verification confirms before deletion is authorized.

summary_for_owner.deletion_authority_status: Owner-decision-required for all 12 candidates. None classified STALE-DUPLICATE (which would have indicated 'candidate-with-no-further-inspection-required per strict path-relative subset proof') because every paired entry has at least one outside-only path under Codex GO condition 7 strict criteria. Every recommendation below uses owner-authorization-gated language; the manifest does not declare any entry deletion-ready independently of owner action.
summary_for_owner.tier_1_rationale: These 5 entries are either ORPHAN with no in-root counterpart and no live-code references in non-bridge directories (per documented grep evidence in evidence_summary fields), or DIVERGED with content that strongly pattern-matches old build residue (widget). Owner may authorize deletion after minimal inspection.
summary_for_owner.tier_2_rationale: These 4 entries warrant a quick owner spot-check: Dockerfile and requirements.txt are 5-second content-diffs against in-root; config has 1 outside-only file to glance at; tmp is recently modified (2026-04-24) so contents should be eyeballed before deletion is authorized.
summary_for_owner.tier_3_rationale: admin (29 outside-only paths, mostly nested-dist artifacts but worth confirming) and src (151 outside-only paths including a non-nested chat/pipeline.py worth spot-checking) need substantive review before deletion. Camtasia is a third-party screen-recording tool install (1.22 GB) — retention is a 'do you still use Camtasia' question, not a content question.
summary_for_owner.language_discipline: Per Codex condition 7 + F3 of -004 NO-GO, no recommendation uses unqualified deletion-safety phrasing for any non-STALE-DUPLICATE entry. All deletion suggestions are gated on owner authorization following spot-check or full inspection per tier.

reproducibility_commands.live_code_reference_grep: grep -rn '<term>' --include='*.py' --include='*.toml' --include='*.json' --include='*.yml' --include='*.yaml' --include='*.ts' --include='*.tsx' --include='*.js' --include='*.jsx' --include='*.md' --include='*.ps1' --include='*.sh' --include='*.bat' E:/GT-KB | grep -v '^./bridge/' | grep -v '^./.venv/' | grep -v '^./node_modules/' | grep -v '^./.codex_pydeps/' | grep -v '^./__pycache__/'
